zone_6b_notes: Plant bare-root canes in early spring (late March to April) in Farmington. Two types: summer-bearing (one crop in June–July from floricanes) and fall-bearing (everbearing; crop in August–October on primocanes). Fall-bearing types like Heritage and Autumn Britten are easier to manage in Missouri — mow all canes to ground in late winter and harvest on new growth each fall. Summer-bearing needs selective pruning (remove fruiting canes after harvest, keep new canes). Needs 4–6 feet of space per row; plant with 8-foot wire trellis. Highly productive once established. Zone 6b winters are generally within raspberry's hardiness range.

planting.best_planting_time: Plant dormant canes late March to April
